ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for salmon shish kebab; closest parent 'kebab food product' (FOODON_03302051) used as the nearest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for salmon shish kebab; 'Salmon, grilled' (A0FKP) is the closest defensible parent covering the primary ingredient and preparation method.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for salmon shish kebab or a sufficiently close parent food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of salmon shish kebab consists of approximately 250 g of grilled salmon pieces on skewers, often accompanied by rice, flatbread, or salad.

nutrient_source.title: Fish, salmon, Atlantic, farmed, cooked, dry heat — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 15237)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are based on USDA SR Legacy data for Atlantic farmed salmon cooked by dry heat, which closely represents the grilled salmon fillet used in shish kebab. A small upward adjustment to sodium (~10 mg/100 g) is applied to account for typical marinade seasoning (salt, spices). Olive oil and lemon marinade contribute negligible macro change at typical usage levels. This is the best available approved-source match for grilled salmon kebab.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pure protein and fat foods such as plain grilled fish contain no glycemic carbohydrates. Per Veritas v2 rules for zero-GI foods, `glycemic_index` is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this food contains no glycemic carbohydrates; `gi_category` is 'low' per convention. Carbohydrate_g per 100 g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — salmon is a protein-fat food with no starch or sugar). Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (0 × 250 / 100) / 100 = 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content with beneficial omega-3 fatty acids
hypertension.guidance: Salmon shish kebab is low in sodium (~69 mg/100 g) and rich in omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids, which are associated with modest blood pressure reduction; individuals with hypertension can include this dish regularly while monitoring any added salt in marinades or accompaniments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates; high-quality protein and omega-3 fats
type2_diabetes.guidance: Salmon shish kebab contains no glycemic carbohydrates (GI = 1, GL = 0 per serving), making it an excellent protein source for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; the high omega-3 content may also support insulin sensitivity, and the dish does not require carbohydrate counting when served without rice or bread.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High omega-3 (EPA/DHA) content from Atlantic salmon
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Grilled salmon is one of the richest dietary sources of long-chain omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA), which are clinically associated with reduced triglycerides, anti-inflammatory effects, and reduced cardiovascular event risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease are encouraged to consume oily fish such as salmon at least twice weekly per AHA guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salmon shish kebab (Turkish: Somon Şiş) is a Turkish grilled fish dish consisting of fresh Atlantic salmon fillets cut into cubes, marinated in a blend of olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, cumin, paprika, and fresh herbs such as parsley and dill, then threaded onto metal or wooden skewers and grilled over charcoal or an open flame until lightly charred on the outside and moist within. The dish is a seafood variant of the classic Turkish şiş kebab tradition, which typically uses lamb or chicken. Salmon shish is prized for its rich, buttery flavour, tender flaky texture, and the smoky aroma imparted by the grill. In Singapore, it is served at Turkish and Lebanese restaurants such as Sultan Palace at Boat Quay, typically accompanied by saffron rice, grilled vegetables, flatbread (pide or lavash), and a yoghurt-based sauce or tahini. Nutritionally, salmon shish kebab is an excellent source of high-quality complete protein (~20 g/100 g), long-chain omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA), vitamin D (~11 mcg/100 g), vitamin B12 (~3.2 mcg/100 g), selenium, and niacin. It contains zero glycemic carbohydrates, making it suitable for low-carbohydrate, diabetic-friendly, and ketogenic dietary patterns. The dish is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. Clinically, regular consumption of oily fish such as salmon is associated with reduced cardiovascular risk, improved lipid profiles, and anti-inflammatory benefits. The dish is halal-certified in most Turkish restaurant contexts in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in salmon shish?
Is salmon shish good for diabetics?
Is salmon shish kebab gluten free?

confidence_reason: No exact match for salmon shish kebab exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy data for Atlantic farmed salmon cooked by dry heat (NDB 15237 / FDC 175168), which is the closest approved-source match. A minor sodium adjustment accounts for marinade seasoning. GI is assigned as 1 (low) based on the established convention for pure protein-fat foods with no glycemic carbohydrates, consistent with the University of Sydney GI Database framework and Veritas v2 rules for zero-GI foods.
notes: 1. Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero: salmon is a protein-fat food containing no starch, sugar, or dietary fibre in the flesh itself; the marinade ingredients (lemon juice, spices) contribute negligible carbohydrate at typical usage levels — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy. 2. Sugar_g is confirmed zero per USDA source for plain grilled salmon. 3. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per USDA source for plain grilled salmon flesh. 4. Trans_fat_g is 0.0; salmon contains negligible industrially produced trans fats — confirmed zero per USDA source. 5. Glycemic index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this food contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category is 'low' per convention, as required by Veritas v2 rules for zero-GI foods. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all reported from USDA SR Legacy (FDC 175168) for Atlantic farmed salmon cooked dry heat. 7. This is a Turkish cuisine dish; multilingual naming rules for Turkish cuisine do not mandate Chinese, Malay, Tamil, or Hindi names. Chinese simplified and traditional names are provided as supplementary aliases for Singapore market context. Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local_names are null as these are not mandatory for Turkish cuisine under G6. 8. FoodOn code FOODON_03302051 and FoodEx2 code A0FKP are used as closest_parent matches; no exact ontology code exists for salmon shish kebab. 9. Serving size of 250 g is an estimate for a typical restaurant portion of 2–3 skewers; actual restaurant portions may vary.
